mainEntity.name: Is it too late to file for hearing loss if I've been working in noise for 10 years and just now noticed it?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is likely not too late, as the deadline usually starts when you realize the hearing loss is work-related. However, you must act quickly once diagnosed to stay within the statute of limitations.
acceptedAnswer.description: In many states, the deadline is 180 days to one year from the date you became aware of the hearing loss and its connection to work. Because hearing loss is gradual, identifying that "awareness" date is the central battleground of these cases. If you wait years after noticing the ringing, the insurance company will argue the "Statute of Limitations" has expired. In Idaho, specific rules apply to how long you must have worked in "hazardous noise" before a claim is even valid. If you've been retired for several years, filing becomes significantly more difficult but not always impossible. Speed is critical to prevent a "denial for lateness."
